description: Use this API to define an unbalanced fault at a bus in dynamic simulations. This API routine was first introduced in release 35.5.0. ierr = dist_def_scmu_fault_3(options, values, disttime)

parameters.name: options
parameters.description: An array of six elements specifying options and values. The first two elements of OPTIONS define options to be used in the construction of the sequence admittance matrices; the remaining entries define the fault to be applied. (input).

parameters.name: values
parameters.description: An array of four elements specifying values used to define the fault. They are dependent on the value specified for OPTIONS(3). (input).

parameters.name: disttime
parameters.description: Time at which the fault is applied in seconds. (input; 1.0 by default).

options.description: dc line and FACTS device option (0 by default).
transformer impedance correction option (1 by default).
do not apply to zero sequence.
bus to be faulted (no default allowed).

node number of a substation node connected to the specified bus; this defines the bus section to be faulted. If 0 is specified for a bus in a substation, the ‘parent’ bus is faulted; 0 must be specified for a bus that is not in a substation (0 by default).

values.description: resistance of the complex L-G (if OPTIONS(3) is 1) or L-L (if OPTIONS(3) is 2) fault impedance in pu (0.0 by default).
reactance of the complex L-G (if OPTIONS(3) is 1) or L-L (if OPTIONS(3) is 2) fault impedance in pu (0.0 by default).
resistance of the complex L-G fault impedance in pu (if OPTIONS(3) is 2) (0.0 by default).
reactance of the complex L-G fault impedance in pu (if OPTIONS(3) is 2) (0.0 by default).

error_codes.description: prerequisite requirements for API are not met.
bus does not belong to a substation.
node not found in the substation of this bus.
node is not connected to any bus section of this bus.
